name: Clive Davis
abstract: Clive Jay Davis (April 4, 1932 – June 22, 2026) was an American record executive, A&R executive, record producer and lawyer. He won four Grammy Awards and was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame as a non-performer in 2000.

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: Clive Davis... signed a production deal... He'll sign you... Clive stuck to his game and I agreed... Clive had me to a meeting in his office... Clive was in the studio. He loved it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: He was determined to make me a star... He had to fight for that... from Arista Records.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: After I did “I Love the Night Life” the next person to call me was Clive Davis and flew me to New York... he loved four of them... I’m flying you tonight to Los Angeles to meet with Melissa Manchester to do her next album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t know him well then. Somebody suggested to him, this is a group that should be on our label. So, “Ramsey, this is Clive Davis.” From that point on, I think I might have seen him two or three times.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was Clive Davis, they decided they wanted it strictly disco.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022
